title: ALP Build Plan v1
description: The machine-readable build plan `scripts/alp_orchestrate/buildplan.py::emit_build_plan` renders for a resolved board.yaml project (the 'Wave C' consumer contract, settled 2026-06-04 with the alp-sdk-vscode team; the real consumer today is `tan`, alplabai/tan-cli). One entry per non-`off` core slice, each carrying its build command, config artefact contents, and app source dir, plus the shared (cross-slice) generated artefacts and any non-fatal planning warnings. Every artefact carries its full `contents` so the consumer's materialise step stays pure IO -- the plan and the Orchestrator's own on-disk materialise step are written from the same `_shared_artefacts`/`_slice_config_artefact` helpers and MUST agree byte-for-byte. camelCase keys (this is a CLI-consumer contract, not an in-repo YAML). Stability: schemaVersion 1 is locked with the tan-cli consumer -- bump schemaVersion and record the change in the CHANGELOG before altering this shape; do not add breaking changes silently.

schemaVersion.description: Build-plan contract version. Independent of board.yaml's own schema_version.

generatedBy.description: Identifies the emitting tool (currently the literal 'scripts/alp_orchestrate.py'). Informational only -- not part of the locked consumer contract.

sdkVersion.description: The `version:` field from metadata/sdk_version.yaml at emit time -- the single source of truth for the SDK release version. Additive, schemaVersion 1 (no bump). Informational provenance, not part of the locked consumer contract.

sdkCommit.description: Short git commit (`git rev-parse --short HEAD`) of the alp-sdk checkout that produced this plan. null when git or the .git directory isn't available (e.g. a wheel-installed CLI with no checkout) -- never fails the emit. Additive, schemaVersion 1 (no bump). Informational provenance, not part of the locked consumer contract.

boardYaml.description: POSIX-style path to the board.yaml this plan was resolved from, as passed to emit_build_plan (issue #596: every relative path elsewhere in the plan is anchored on this file's own directory, never the emitting process's CWD).

buildRoot.description: POSIX-style path to the root build directory every slice's buildDir and the shared artefacts are nested under.

executionPolicy.description: SDK-owned skip-vs-fail policy the executor MUST apply when dispatching a slice's `command`, so a plan consumer stops hand-porting that logic. Additive, schemaVersion 1.

unknownBackend.description: Outcome when a slice's `backend` has no known tool mapping. Currently always 'fail'.

missingTool.description: Outcome when `command.tool` isn't found on the executor's PATH. Currently always 'skip' (normal on a dev host missing one toolchain).

nullCommand.description: Outcome when a slice's `command` is null (this emitter had no valid command to produce -- see `slices[].command`). Currently always 'skip'.

slices.description: One entry per core whose resolved os is not `off`, sorted by coreId. A core this emitter cannot yet produce a build command for (e.g. missing app/board/image, or an app-only Yocto slice with no recipe:) is still carried here with command: null plus a matching entry in `warnings` -- never silently dropped.

backend.description: The slice's resolved os (Slice.os). `off` cores never reach the plan, so this enum excludes it.

appDir.description: Resolved absolute (POSIX-style) path to this slice's app source directory, independent of `command` -- lets tooling watch the app source without reverse-engineering it out of a yocto/zephyr/baremetal-shaped command (issue #597). null when the slice has no app source dir to report, e.g. a Yocto slice built from the `alp-image-edge` stock-image token rather than an `app:` path.

configArtefacts.description: This slice's own generated config artefact(s) (alp.conf / local.conf / cmake-args.txt), or an empty array when the slice's os has none. Byte-identical to what the Orchestrator's own materialise step writes to buildDir.

path.description: POSIX-style path (under buildDir) the consumer should write `contents` to.

contents.description: Full file contents, ready to byte-write verbatim.

toolchain.description: This slice's compiler identity (#610 §4 per-slice tooling index), grounded in the SoM preset's `topology.<core>.toolchain` -- never invented. A field genuinely not derivable for this slice's runtime is null rather than guessed (e.g. a Yocto slice's exact GCC triple depends on the Yocto build's own TUNE_FEATURES/TCLIBC, outside board.yaml/SoM metadata).

targetTriple.description: GCC target triple for this slice's compiler (e.g. `arm-zephyr-eabi` for a Zephyr Cortex-M slice, which is both the Zephyr SDK toolchain directory name and its real GCC triple). null when not derivable (e.g. Yocto -- the triple depends on the Yocto build's own TCLIBC/TUNE_FEATURES).

compiler.description: Compiler executable name for this slice (e.g. `arm-zephyr-eabi-gcc`). null when not derivable.

sysroot.description: Cross-compile sysroot path for this slice, when the runtime has one. Always null today -- Zephyr bundles its own libc per architecture inside the Zephyr SDK (no conventional sysroot), and Yocto's sysroot lives inside the Yocto build tree, not in board.yaml/SoM metadata.

id.description: Stable toolchain identifier, taken verbatim from the SoM preset's `topology.<core>.toolchain` (e.g. `arm-zephyr-eabi`, `poky-glibc`) -- the same field `system-manifest.yaml`'s `slices[].toolchain` already surfaces. null when the SoM preset doesn't declare one for this core.

artifacts.description: Deterministic OUTPUT paths under `buildDir` for this slice (#610 §4) -- the WHERE, not a promise the files exist yet (they don't until the slice is actually built). Zephyr's own CMake layout fixes these paths (cmake/modules/kernel.cmake + the top-level CMakeLists.txt); a Yocto slice's real output lands under the Yocto build tree's own deploy dir (outside this slice's buildDir), and a baremetal slice's executable name is the app's own CMakeLists.txt to pick -- both stay null rather than guess a path this emitter can't predict.

elf.description: POSIX-style path to the slice's ELF output (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/zephyr/zephyr.elf`). null when this runtime has no single predictable ELF output (yocto, baremetal).

map.description: POSIX-style path to the linker map file (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/zephyr/zephyr.map`). null when not derivable.

bin.description: POSIX-style path to the flat binary image (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/zephyr/zephyr.bin`). null when not derivable.

sizeReport.description: POSIX-style path to the readelf-headers size/stat report (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/zephyr/zephyr.stat`, written only when `CONFIG_OUTPUT_STAT=y` -- same 'doesn't exist until built/enabled' caveat as every other artifact path here). null when not derivable.

symbols.description: POSIX-style path to the symbol-table dump (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/zephyr/zephyr.symbols`, written only when `CONFIG_OUTPUT_SYMBOLS=y`). null when not derivable.

compileCommands.description: POSIX-style path to `compile_commands.json` (Zephyr: `<buildDir>/compile_commands.json` -- the top-level Zephyr CMakeLists.txt forces `CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS` unconditionally). null when the slice's runtime doesn't produce one at a predictable path.

debug.description: Headless monitor/debug selectors for this slice (#610 §4): the console device selector + debug-probe selector a headless consumer needs, derived from board.yaml `diagnostics.console:` and the same flash-recipe fact `system-manifest.yaml`'s `slices[].flash_method`/`flash_args` already carry. null when unknown.

console.description: Resolved console backend selector: `uart` (Zephyr UART console), `ram` (Zephyr RAM console, read over SWD), or `linux` (Yocto/Ubuntu stdio/tty console). null when the slice inherits an unspecified board default (`diagnostics.console: none`/unset with no OS-derived default, e.g. a baremetal slice).

probe.description: Debug-probe runner selector for this slice: the explicitly-configured runner for a Zephyr slice's `zephyr_west_flash` recipe, else null -- meaning either the board.cmake default runner applies, or (Yocto image-flash, baremetal cmake-flash) this slice's runtime/flash recipe doesn't identify a live debug probe at all.

command.description: The build command to run for this slice, or null when this emitter has no valid command yet (see `slices` description; a matching `warnings` entry explains why).

cwd.description: POSIX-style working directory to run `tool` from; always equal to this slice's buildDir.

env.description: Environment variables the consumer must inject into the slice subprocess, native host-path form (handed to the subprocess environment verbatim).

ALP_SDK_ROOT.description: Absolute host path to the alp-sdk checkout the plan was generated from.

envAppendPath.description: SDK-owned env values the consumer must APPEND, skipping any value already present (de-dup first, matching `_alp_common.env_with_sdk` / `_workspace.subprocess_env`), to the slice subprocess env, distinct from `env` (set-verbatim). The join separator is PER-KEY, not uniformly os.pathsep: `EXTRA_ZEPHYR_MODULES` is a CMake list -- Zephyr's `zephyr_module.py` splits it on `;` on every platform, never an OS path list -- while `PYTHONPATH` is a real OS-native path list joined with `os.pathsep`. Plan wins / CLI fills gaps: a consumer that computes these itself should prefer the plan's values. Additive under schemaVersion 1; a new key so consumers pinned to an older shape safely ignore it.

sharedArtefacts.description: Cross-slice generated artefacts (e.g. generated/alp/system_ipc.h, dts-reservations.dtsi, dts-partitions.dtsi, and -- only when the project declares them -- alp_sysbuild.conf / sysbuild/tfm/tfm.conf). Conditional artefacts follow absence-emits-nothing: they appear only when their emit is non-empty. Byte-identical to what the Orchestrator's own materialise step writes under buildRoot.

path.description: POSIX-style path (under buildRoot) the consumer should write `contents` to.

contents.description: Full file contents, ready to byte-write verbatim.

warnings.description: Non-fatal planning warnings, one per affected core, machine-readable (`code`) plus a human-readable `message` so the consumer can surface -- or filter -- them without string-matching prose.

code.description: Stable machine-readable warning code. Known values as of schemaVersion 1: 'no-command' (missing app/board/image for the core's os), 'yocto-recipe-missing' (an app-only Yocto slice has app: but no recipe:, so there is no valid bitbake target). New codes may be added without a schemaVersion bump -- consumers must not treat this as a closed enum.

coreId.description: The core this warning applies to (matches a `slices[].coreId`).

message.description: Human-readable explanation, safe to display verbatim.
